name: Barbacoa Mexicana (Barbecue from Mexico City)
recipeIngredient: Lamb pieces (backbone, legs, shoulders, ribs, head)
recipeInstructions:
1. Split the lamb into sections: backbone, legs, shoulders, ribs, and head.
2. Dig a hole about four feet by two feet across for the pit.
3. Gather kindling and clean maguey leaves for lining the pit.
4. Place large porous stones in the bottom of the pit (avoid small hard rocks).
5. Build a fire with dry wood until it burns to smokeless coal stage.
6. Toast maguey leaves until limp to release juices for seasoning the meat.
7. Line the pit with maguey leaves, ensuring all mud is covered and meat won't stick.
8. Place a grate in the pit and arrange soup ingredients (consomme) on top.
9. Arrange lamb pieces on the casserole starting with backbone, then legs, shoulders, ribs, and head.
10. Do not salt the meat before cooking to prevent toughness.
11. Fold maguey leaf tips into the pit to cover the meat well.
12. Cover with a metal sheet and seal with fresh mud.
13. Maintain fire for 5-6 hours depending on meat age.
14. Salt the meat before serving and accompany with Salsa Borracha.
15. Serve broth in small cups.
See Shepherd's Roast with Drunken Sauce for recipe.
From "The Art of Mexican Cooking" by Jan Aaron and Sachs Salom. Doubleday and Company, N.Y., 1965.
